matches.match_rationale: The core pain is deciding how to split shared costs across multiple grants and campuses in a defensible, auditable way. This is a constrained resource allocation problem.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A grant allocation optimizer that ingests shared staff records, utility bills, and campus-grant funding mixes, then proposes allocation splits with documented rationale.

matches.match_rationale: Audit documentation is a direct output requirement of grant compliance — finance teams must produce written allocation methodology narratives.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An allocation memo generator that takes finalized cost splits and grant parameters as inputs and produces audit-ready narrative documentation.

matches.match_rationale: A major downstream risk of misallocation is auditor-identified ineligible expenses charged to a government grant — triggering clawback. An anomaly detection layer can flag likely misclassified expense line items before submission.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A pre-submission expense screening tool that compares each coded expense against the grant allowable-use rules and flags probable ineligible allocations.

matches.match_rationale: The allocation spreadsheets contain implicit rules: partial-cost allocation formulas, campus-grant mapping tables, and eligibility filters scattered across tabs and versions.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A pipeline that ingests the existing allocation spreadsheets and outputs a structured allocation ruleset — grant codes, eligible expense categories, shared-cost apportionment formulas.

matches.match_rationale: The monthly allocation workflow is a multi-step process: pull shared cost actuals, identify which grants apply per campus, apply apportionment rules, produce draft allocations, generate documentation, and flag exceptions.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An end-to-end grant allocation workflow agent that runs monthly: pulls actuals, applies the allocation ruleset, produces draft splits, flags anomalies, and generates the audit memo.
